requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Payments Banks -
Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Payments Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
requirements.object: immediate effect

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Payments Banks (hereinafter collectively
referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
requirements.object: Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of the provisions of section 29 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, a
bank shall in respect of all business transacted by it prepare a balance sheet and
profit and loss account as on the last working day of the year or the period, as
the case may be, in the Forms set out in the Third Schedule of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: business transacted by it prepare a balance sheet and profit and loss account as on the last working day of the year or the period,

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall follow the general instructions for the compilation of balance sheet
and profit and loss account as specified in subparagraph (1) below.
requirements.object: for the compilation of balance sheet and profit and loss account as specified in subparagraph (1) below

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall
ensure strict compliance with the Accounting Standards notified under the
Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules, 2021, as amended from time to time,
subject to Directions / Guidelines issued by the RBI.
requirements.object: the Accounting Standards notified under the Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules, 2021, as amended from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: Mere mention of an activity, transaction, or item in instructions for
compilation does not imply that it is permitted, and the bank shall refer to the
extant statutory and regulatory requirements while determining the permissibility
or otherwise of an activity or transaction.
requirements.object: statutory and regulatory requirements while determining the permissibility or otherwise of an activity or transaction

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall also be guided by the following with respect to relevant issues in the
application of certain Accounting Standards for the bank:
(1) Accounting Standard 5 - Net Profit or Loss for the Period, Prior Period
Items and Changes in Accounting Policies
requirements.object: the following with respect to relevant issues in the application of certain Accounting Standards for the bank: (1) Accounting Standa

conditions:
if the income / expenditure is reckoned on a gross basis
or one per cent of the net profit before taxes or net losses
requirements.full_sentence:
Since materiality is not objectively defined, it has been
decided that all banks should ensure compliance with the provisions of the
Accounting Standard in respect of any item of prior period income or prior
period expenditure which exceeds one per cent of the total income / total
expenditure of the bank if the income / expenditure is reckoned on a gross basis
or one per cent of the net profit before taxes or net losses, as the case may be
if the income is reckoned net of costs.
requirements.object: is reckoned net of costs

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Since the format of the profit and loss accounts of a bank prescribed in Form
B under Third Schedule to the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 does not
<image>
specifically provide for disclosure of the impact of prior period items on the
current year's profit and loss, such disclosures, wherever warranted, may be
made in the 'Notes on Accounts' to the balance sheet of a bank.
requirements.object: 'Notes on Accounts' to the balance sheet of a bank

conditions:
in case of non-performing advances
and non-performing investments
requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Non-recognition of income by the bank in case of non-performing advances
and non-performing investments, in compliance with the regulatory
prescriptions of the RBI, shall not attract a qualification by the statutory auditors
as this would be in conformity with provisions of the standard, as it recognises
postponement of recognition of revenue where collectability of the revenue is
significantly uncertain.
requirements.object: the statutory auditors as this would be in conformity with provisions of the standard, as it recognises postponement of rec

requirements.full_sentence:
The issues that arise in this context have been identified and a bank
shall be guided by the following while complying with the provisions of the
standard:
requirements.object: following while complying with the provisions of the standard:

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) As per paragraphs 9 and 21 of the Standard, a foreign currency transaction
shall be recorded, on initial recognition in the reporting currency, by applying to
the foreign currency amount the exchange rate between the reporting currency
and the foreign currency at the date of the transaction.
requirements.object: recognition in the reporting currency, by applying to the foreign currency amount the exchange rate between the reporting curr

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may face
difficulty in applying the exchange rate prevailing at the date of the transaction
in respect of the items which are not being recorded in Indian Rupees or are
currently being recorded using a notional exchange rate.
requirements.object: the exchange rate prevailing at the date of the transaction in respect of the items which are not being recorded in Indian

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) A bank, which is in a position to apply the exchange rate prevailing on the date
of the transaction for recording the foreign currency transactions as required
under AS 11 shall comply with the requirements.

A bank, which has an
extensive branch network, have a high volume of foreign currency transactions,
and is not fully equipped on the technology front shall be guided by the
following:
requirements.object: following:

requirements.full_sentence:
In view of the practical difficulties
which a bank may have in applying the exchange rates at the dates of the
transactions, and since the Standard allows the use of a rate that
approximates the actual rate at the date of the transaction, the bank may
use average rates as detailed below:
requirements.object: exchange rates at the dates of the transactions, and since the Standard allows the use of a rate that approximates the actual rat

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) In respect of those foreign currency transactions, which are currently not
being recorded in Indian Rupees at the date of the transaction, or are being
recorded using a notional exchange rate shall now be recorded at the date
of the transaction by using the weekly average closing rate of the preceding
week, published by FEDAI, if the same approximates the actual rate at the
date of the transaction.
requirements.object: the date of the transaction by using the weekly average closing rate of the preceding week, published by FEDAI

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) If the weekly average closing rate of the preceding week does not
approximate the actual rate at the date of the transaction, the closing rate
at the date of the transaction shall be used.

A bank shall, at the minimum, furnish the following information in the 'Notes to
Accounts'.
requirements.object: furnish the following information in the 'Notes to Accounts'

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall note that mere mention of an activity, transaction or
item in the disclosure template does not imply that it is permitted, and the bank
shall refer to the extant statutory and regulatory requirements while determining
the permissibility or otherwise of an activity or transaction.
requirements.object: of an activity, transaction or item in the disclosure template does not imply that it is permitted, and the bank shall refer to

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall
disclose comparative information in respect of the previous period for all amounts
reported in the current period's financial statements.
requirements.object: respect of the previous period for all amounts reported in the current period's financial statements

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the bank shall
include comparative information for narrative and descriptive information if it is
relevant to understanding the current period's financial statements.
requirements.object: narrative and descriptive information if it is relevant to understanding the current period's financial statements

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(1) Regulatory capital
Note: A bank shall be guided by the Reserve Bank of India (Payment Banks - Asset Liability
Management) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to time, for associated guidelines on asset
liability-management.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Payment Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to time, for associated gui

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall also specify if the instruments are Basel II or Basel III compliant.
requirements.object: instruments are Basel II or Basel III compliant

### ###

Total investments (I+II)

Note: This disclosure shall exclude Level 3 assets where the valuation of the asset is the price
declared by FBIL / FIMMDA for that asset.
requirements.object: where the valuation of the asset is the price declared by FBIL / FIMMDA for that asset

requirements.full_sentence:
For a bank, the Total under column 3 shall match with the sum of total of Investments included under the following categories in Schedule 8 to the balance sheet:
a) Investment in India in
i) Shares ii) Debentures and Bonds iii) Subsidiaries and / or Joint Ventures iv) Others
b) Investment outside India in (where applicable)
i) Government securities (including local authorities) ii) Subsidiaries and / or joint ventures abroad iii) Other investments
requirements.object: of total of Investments included under the following categories in Schedule 8 to the balance sheet: a) Investment in India in i) Sh

requirements.full_sentence: Amounts reported under columns 4, 5, 6 and 7 above may not be mutually exclusive.

Note:
Note: The disclosure shall be as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (Government Securities
Lending) Directions, 2023, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank of India (Government Securities Lending) Directions, 2023, as amended from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Fraud accounts
A bank shall disclose details the number and amount of frauds as well as the
provisioning thereon as per template given below.
requirements.object: and amount of frauds as well as the provisioning thereon as per template given below

conditions: If a bank has no exposure to country risk in both the current and previous year
requirements.full_sentence:
ii) Other (please specify)
Total exposure to capital market
Note: If a bank has no exposure to country risk in both the current and previous year, it may omit disclosure of the table while mentioning that it has no exposure to country risk.
requirements.object: table while mentioning that it has no exposure to country risk

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Intra-group exposures: A bank shall make the following disclosures for the
current year with comparatives for the previous year:
Note: Nature and terms of the swaps including information on credit and market risk and the accounting
policies adopted for recording the swaps shall also be disclosed.
requirements.object: for the current year with comparatives for the previous year: Note: Nature and terms of the swaps including information

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Penalties imposed by the RBI under the provisions of the (a) Banking
Regulation Act, 1949, (b) Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007, and (iii)
Government Securities Act, 2006 (for bouncing of SGL) shall be disclosed in the
'Notes to Accounts' to the balance sheet in the concerned bank's next Annual
Report.
requirements.object: 'Notes to Accounts' to the balance sheet in the concerned bank's next Annual Report

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) A bank shall make appropriate disclosures on the nature of the breach, number
of instances of default and the quantum of penalty imposed.
requirements.object: the nature of the breach, number of instances of default and the quantum of penalty imposed

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The defaulting participant in a reverse repo transaction shall make appropriate
disclosure on the number of instances of default as well as the quantum of penalty
paid to the RBI during the financial year.
requirements.object: the number of instances of default as well as the quantum of penalty paid to the RBI during the financial year

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) A bank is required to make disclosure on remuneration of Whole Time
Directors / Chief Executive Officers / Material Risk Takers on an annual basis at
the minimum, in its Annual Financial Statements.
requirements.object: remuneration of Whole Time Directors / Chief Executive Officers / Material Risk Takers on an annual basis at the minimum, in its

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The bank shall make the disclosures in table or chart format and make
disclosures for previous as well as the current reporting year.
requirements.object: table or chart format and make disclosures for previous as well as the current reporting year

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Further, a bank (to the extent applicable), shall disclose the following information:
Type of disclosure

Information
Qualitative
Information relating to the composition and mandate of the Nomination and
(a)
Remuneration Committee.
requirements.object: Type of disclosure Information Qualitative Information relating to the composition and mandate of the Nomination

requirements.full_sentence:
It should include the nature and type of the key measures
used to take account of these risks.
requirements.object: type of the key measures used to take account of these risks

requirements.full_sentence:
disclosures should only
cover Whole Time

(iv) A bank shall also disclose remuneration paid to the non-executive directors on an
annual basis at the minimum, in its Annual Financial Statements.
requirements.object: to the non-executive directors on an annual basis at the minimum, in its Annual Financial Statements

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) Share-linked instruments should be fair valued on the date of grant by the bank
using Black-Scholes model.
requirements.object: the date of grant by the bank using Black-Scholes model

requirements.full_sentence:
The fair value thus arrived at should be recognised as
an expense beginning with the accounting period for which approval has been
granted.
requirements.object: expense beginning with the accounting period for which approval has been granted

requirements.full_sentence:
4 For the purpose of computation of business per employee (deposits plus advances), inter-bank deposits
shall be excluded.

(ii) Bancassurance business: The details of fees / brokerage earned in respect
of insurance broking, agency and bancassurance business undertaken by a bank
shall be disclosed for both the current year and previous year.
requirements.object: the current year and previous year

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Marketing and distribution: A bank shall disclose the details of fees /
remuneration received in respect of the marketing and distribution function
(excluding bancassurance business) undertaken by it.
requirements.object: fees / remuneration received in respect of the marketing and distribution function (excluding bancassurance business) undertak

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) However, a bank shall continue to disclose the strategy for Ind AS
implementation, including the progress made in this regard.
requirements.object: strategy for Ind AS implementation, including the progress made in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
These disclosures
shall be made until implementation of Ind AS.
requirements.object: of Ind AS

requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) Payment of DICGC Insurance Premium
1[A bank shall disclose in the annual report that 'deposit insurance premium as
applicable was paid to DICGC within the prescribed timelines'.
requirements.object: report that 'deposit insurance premium as applicable was paid to DICGC within the prescribed timelines'

conditions:
In case the bank
has not paid as per the required timelines
requirements.full_sentence:
In case the bank
has not paid as per the required timelines, the same shall also be disclosed.]
1 Substituted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Payments Banks - Financial Statements:
Presentation and Disclosures) -Amendment Directions, 2026 dated March 16, 2026.

In addition to standalone financial statements prepared as per the formats
prescribed under Section 29 of Banking Regulation Act, 1949, a bank, whether
listed or unlisted, shall prepare and disclose CFS in its Annual Reports, in the
formats prescribed in Annex II.
requirements.object: in its Annual Reports, in the formats prescribed in Annex II

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The CFS shall normally include a consolidated balance sheet,
consolidated statement of profit and loss, principal accounting policies, and 'Notes
to Accounts'.
requirements.object: balance sheet, consolidated statement of profit and loss, principal accounting policies, and 'Notes to Accounts'

requirements.full_sentence:
The CFS for the bank shall also be submitted to the Department of Supervision
(DoS), RBI within one month from the publication of the bank's annual accounts.
requirements.object: the Department of Supervision (DoS), RBI within one month from the publication of the bank's annual accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
CFS shall be prepared in terms of the applicable accounting standards for the
bank.
requirements.object: of the applicable accounting standards for the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation 1: For the purpose of financial reporting, the terms 'parent', 'associate',
'joint venture', 'control', and 'group' shall have the same meaning as ascribed to
them in the applicable accounting standards for the bank.
requirements.object: as ascribed to them in the applicable accounting standards for the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation 2: An RRB shall be treated as an 'associate' in the CFS of its sponsor
bank.
requirements.object: 'associate' in the CFS of its sponsor bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The responsibility of determining whether a particular entity shall be included or
not for consolidation would be that of the Management of the parent entity.
requirements.object: for consolidation would be that of the Management of the parent entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, the Statutory Auditors shall mention in their audit report, if they are
of the opinion that an entity which ought to have been consolidated has been
omitted.
requirements.object: report

conditions:
where different entities in a group are governed by different accounting
norms laid down by the concerned regulator(s)
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where different entities in a group are governed by different accounting
norms laid down by the concerned regulator(s), the balance sheet size may be
used to determine the dominant activity and accounting norms specified by its
regulator may be used for the consolidation of similar transactions and events.
requirements.object: the dominant activity and accounting norms specified by its regulator may be used for the consolidation of similar transactions a

conditions: where banking is the dominant activity
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, where banking is the dominant activity, accounting norms applicable
to a bank shall be used for consolidation purposes in respect of like transactions
and other events in similar circumstances.
requirements.object: purposes in respect of like transactions and other events in similar circumstances

requirements.full_sentence:
The valuation of investments by the bank in associates that are not included using
the 'Equity Method' shall be as per the relevant valuation norms issued by the RBI.
requirements.object: relevant valuation norms issued by the RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of Directors of the bank shall invariably record the intent of holding the
investment for a temporary period or otherwise at the time of investment in the
associate, joint venture.
requirements.object: of holding the investment for a temporary period or otherwise at the time of investment in the associate, joint venture

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, in the absence of a record of such intent by the Board at the time of
such investment, the investee entity shall be consolidated into the CFS.
requirements.object: CFS

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall adhere to following guidelines for unreconciled inter-branch account
entries.
requirements.object: for unreconciled inter-branch account entries

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) The bank shall segregate the credit entries outstanding for more than five years in
the inter-branch account and transfer them to a separate 'Blocked Account' which
shall be shown under 'Other Liabilities and Provisions - Others'.
requirements.object: outstanding for more than five years in the inter-branch account and transfer them to a separate 'Blocked Account' which

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Any adjustment from the Blocked Account should be permitted only with the
authorisation of two officials, one of whom should be from the Controlling / Head
Office if the amount exceeds ₹ one lakh.
requirements.object: the authorisation of two officials, one of whom should be from the Controlling / Head Office if the amount exceeds ₹ one lakh

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The balance in Blocked Account shall be reckoned as a liability for the purpose of
the maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) and Statutory Liquidity Ratio
(SLR).
requirements.object: liability for the purpose of the maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) and Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR)

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The bank shall maintain category-wise (head-wise) accounts for various types of
transactions put through inter-branch accounts, so that the netting can be done
category-wise.
requirements.object: for various types of transactions put through inter-branch accounts, so that the netting can be done categ

requirements.full_sentence:
As on the balance sheet date, the bank shall segregate the debit
and credit entries remaining unreconciled for more than six months and arrive at
the net position category-wise, while also considering the balance in the Blocked
Account.
requirements.object: credit entries remaining unreconciled for more than six months and arrive at the net position category-wise, while also consid

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The net debit under all the categories of inter-branch accounts shall be aggregated
and a provision equivalent to 100 per cent of the aggregate net debit shall be made.
requirements.object: provision equivalent to 100 per cent of the aggregate net debit shall be made

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, the bank shall ensure that the net debit in one category is not set-
off against net credit in another category.
requirements.object: debit in one category is not set- off against net credit in another category

requirements.full_sentence:
Treatment of outstanding entries in Nostro accounts shall of a bank shall be as
under:
(1) The bank shall take steps to have a strong control over reconciliation and put in
place a system of real-time reconciliation, which provides for immediate escalation
of differences, if any.
requirements.object: be as under: (1) The bank shall take steps to have a strong control over reconciliation and put in place a system of real-time reconciliation, which provides for immediate escalation of differences

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) There shall be close monitoring of pending items in Nostro accounts by top
management at short intervals.
requirements.object: pending items in Nostro accounts by top management at short intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) All unreconciled credit entries in Nostro accounts which are outstanding for more
than three years shall be transferred to a Blocked Account and shown as
outstanding liabilities.
requirements.object: Blocked Account and shown as outstanding liabilities

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The balance in the Blocked Account shall be reckoned for the purpose of CRR /
SLR.
requirements.object: purpose of CRR / SLR

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) A bank shall make 100 per cent provision in respect of all unreconciled debit entries
in the Nostro accounts, which are outstanding for more than two years.
requirements.object: provision in respect of all unreconciled debit entries in the Nostro accounts, which are outstanding for more than two years

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of section 17(1) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 a bank is required to
transfer, out of the balance of profit as disclosed in the profit and loss account, a
sum equivalent to not less than 20 per cent of such profit to Reserve Fund.
requirements.object: the balance of profit as disclosed in the profit and loss account, a sum equivalent to not less than 20 per cent of such profit to

requirements.full_sentence:
However, to augment capital, a
bank shall transfer not less than 25 per cent of the 'net profit' before appropriations
to the Statutory Reserve.
requirements.object: 25 per cent of the 'net profit' before appropriations to the Statutory Reserve

requirements.full_sentence:
Unless specifically allowed by extant regulations, the bank shall take prior approval
from the RBI before any appropriation is made from the Statutory Reserve or any
other reserve.
requirements.object: the RBI before any appropriation is made from the Statutory Reserve or any other reserve

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks are further advised that,
(1) all expenses including provisions and write-offs recognised in a period, whether
mandatory or prudential, shall be reflected in the profit and loss account for the
period as an 'above the line' item (i.e., before arriving at the net profit / loss for the
year);
requirements.object: profit and loss account for the period as an 'above the line' item (i

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) draw down from reserves, with the prior approval of RBI, shall be effected only
'below the line' (i.e.
requirements.object: the line' (i

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(3) suitable disclosures shall be made of such draw down in the 'Notes on Accounts'
to the Balance Sheet.
requirements.object: draw down in the 'Notes on Accounts' to the Balance Sheet

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, the share premium account shall not be utilised for writing off the
expenses relating to the issue of debt instruments.
requirements.object: off the expenses relating to the issue of debt instruments

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: For the purposes of this Direction, issue expenses shall include
registration and other regulatory fees, payments made to legal, accounting, and
other professional advisers, printing costs, and stamp duties.
requirements.object: regulatory fees, payments made to legal, accounting, and other professional advisers, printing costs, and stamp duties

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of provisioning for frauds, a bank that has reported the fraud within the
prescribed time shall have the option to make the provision for the same over a
period, not exceeding four quarters, commencing from the quarter in which the
fraud has been detected.
requirements.object: make the provision for the same over a period, not exceeding four quarters, commencing from the quarter in which the fraud has been

requirements.full_sentence:
Where the bank chooses to provide for the fraud over two to four quarters and this
results in the full provisioning being made in more than one financial year, subject
to compliance with applicable laws, it may debit reserves other than the Statutory
Reserve by the amount remaining un-provided at the end of the financial year by
credit to provisions.
requirements.object: the Statutory Reserve by the amount remaining un-provided at the end of the financial year by credit to provisions

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, it should subsequently reverse the debits to the reserves
proportionately and complete the provisioning by debiting profit and loss account,
in the successive quarters of the next financial year.
requirements.object: reverse the debits to the reserves proportionately and complete the provisioning by debiting profit and loss account, in the

conditions: Where there has been delay
requirements.full_sentence:
Where there has been delay, beyond the prescribed period, in reporting the fraud
to the RBI, the entire provisioning is required to be made at once.
requirements.object: once

requirements.full_sentence:
Unreconciled credit balances in any transitory account representing unclaimed
balances shall not be transferred to the profit and loss account or to any reserves.
requirements.object: profit and loss account or to any reserves

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall make provisions for DTL on the Special Reserve created under
Section 36(1)(viii) of Income Tax Act, 1961.
requirements.object: on the Special Reserve created under Section 36(1)(viii) of Income Tax Act, 1961

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that balance sheet and profit and loss account reflect true and
fair picture of its financial position.
requirements.object: and profit and loss account reflect true and fair picture of its financial position

requirements.full_sentence:
Instances of window dressing of financials, short provisioning, under-reporting /
incorrect computation of exposure / risk weight, incorrect capitalisation of
expenses, deliberate inflation of asset and liabilities at the end of the financial year
and subsequent reversal immediately in next financial year, etc., shall be viewed
seriously and appropriate penal action in terms of the provisions of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949 shall be considered.
requirements.object: appropriate penal action in terms of the provisions of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 shall be considered

requirements.full_sentence:
The
Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these Directions,
instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially affect:
a.
requirements.object: affect: a

conditions: if those directions
requirements.full_sentence:
and
any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time being
in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
<image>
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of
these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
For Banks incorporated outside India Capital (i) The amount brought in by banks by way of start-up capital as prescribed by RBI should be shown under this head.
requirements.object: head

requirements.full_sentence:
Others *
Total
* In case there is any unadjusted balance of loss the same may be shown under this item with appropriate foot-note.
requirements.object: item with appropriate foot-note

requirements.full_sentence:
Miscellaneous Income
Total
Note: Under items II to V loss figures shall be shown in brackets

Appropriations
Transfer to statutory reserves
Transfer to other reserves
Transfer to Government / Proposed dividend
Balance carried over to consolidated balance sheet
Total
Earnings per Share1
1Earning per share shall be for both basic and diluted.
requirements.object: and diluted

requirements.full_sentence:
Schedule 2 - Reserves and Surplus1

As on 31.3.__ (current year)
As on 31.3.__
(previous
year) Statutory Reserves


Capital Reserves


Capital Reserve on Consolidation


Share Premium


Other Reserves (specify nature)


Revenue and other Reserves


Balance in Profit and Loss Account2


Total
1Opening balances, additions and deductions since the last consolidated balance sheet shall be shown under each of
the specified heads.
requirements.object: of the specified heads

requirements.full_sentence: 2In case of loss the balance shall be shown as a deduction.
requirements.object: deduction

conditions: when required by a statute
requirements.full_sentence:
Additional line items, headings and sub-headings shall be presented in the consolidated balance sheet
and consolidated profit and loss account and schedules thereto when required by a statute, Accounting
Standards or when such a presentation is necessary to present the true and fair view of the group's financial
position and operating results.
requirements.object: consolidated balance sheet and consolidated profit and loss account and schedules thereto when required by a statute, Accounting S

requirements.full_sentence:
In the preparation and presentation of CFS Accounting Standards issued by
the ICAI, to the extent applicable to banks, and the guidelines issued by RBI shall be followed.
